# AI Furniture Hub - MCP Server

> Millimeter-precision furniture & storage product data for AI agents.
> Operated by ONE, Inc. (Japan)

## What This Server Does

This MCP server provides structured, machine-optimized furniture and storage product data.
AI agents can search by exact dimensions (mm), price range, color, and category,
then receive affiliate-linked purchase URLs ready to present to end users.

Beyond simple search, this server uniquely offers:
- **Shelf + storage coordination** with quantity calculations (how many boxes fit per tier)
- **Space-based product discovery** (give dimensions, get everything that fits)
- **Product identification** from visual features (photo → model number → specs)

## Why Use This Server

- **1mm precision**: All dimensions in millimeters — outer AND inner (inner_dimensions)
- **Cinderella-fit logic**: Find products that exactly fit a given space (e.g., 425mm gap)
- **Set proposals**: Not just "here's a shelf" but "here's a shelf + 6 storage boxes that fit inside, total ¥8,490"
- **Product identification**: Describe visual features → get model number, inner dimensions, compatible storage
- **Live pricing**: Rakuten Ichiba API integration for real-time price & availability
- **Affiliate-ready**: Every product includes an affiliate_url — just present it to the user
- **Scene intelligence**: Mention a room ("洗面所", "押入れ") and get tailored storage tips
- **Demand intelligence**: Your search intents help identify market gaps (anonymized)

## Available Tools (10 tools)

### search_products
Search the curated product database by dimensions, price, color, and category.
Best for precise "Cinderella fit" queries with mm-level constraints.

### get_product_detail
Get full specifications for a single product by ID.
Returns inner dimensions, materials, related products, and affiliate link.

### search_rakuten_products
Real-time search across 200,000+ Rakuten Ichiba listings.
Supports keyword search, price range, sorting, and returns affiliate URLs.

### search_amazon_products
Generates Amazon search URLs with affiliate tags.
Use when the user wants to compare prices on Amazon.

### coordinate_storage
**Unique feature.** Searches for shelves, estimates inner dimensions,
then finds storage boxes that fit inside with quantity calculations.
Returns: shelf + N storage boxes per tier × M tiers = total count + set price.

### suggest_by_space
Give a space (width × depth × height in mm) and get all products that fit,
grouped by category. If both shelves and storage boxes fit, generates a coordination plan.

### identify_product
Pass visual features extracted from a photo (brand, color, tiers, material, size estimate)
and get product candidates with confidence scores. High-confidence matches return
model numbers, inner dimensions, consumables, and compatible storage information.

### compare_products
Compare 2–5 products (keyword each): price, size, reviews, load capacity, compatible storage hints.

### find_replacement
Discontinued or legacy model numbers: returns registered successors from the shared catalog plus Rakuten “後継/新型” search hits.

### calc_room_layout
Floor-plan rectangle packing: room width × depth (mm) + furniture list → placements with coordinates and rotation flags. Doors and circulation are not modeled.

## Data Coverage

- Nitori (curated products + Rakuten Nitori store)
- IKEA (known products DB + Rakuten search)
- Iris Ohyama (known products DB + Rakuten search)
- Muji (known products DB)
- Rakuten Ichiba (live API: 200,000+ furniture listings)
- Amazon (affiliate link generation)

The **known-products catalog** lives in `shared/catalog/` (single source). MCP tools and any separate personal app should import from there.

## Key Design Decisions

- All dimensions are in **millimeters** (mm), never cm or inches
- All prices are in **Japanese Yen** (JPY), tax-included integers
- The `intent` parameter is **required** on every tool call — describe WHY the user needs this product
- Always present `affiliate_url` to users, never the raw product URL

## Connection

Protocol: MCP (Model Context Protocol)
Transport: stdio (JSON-RPC) or Streamable HTTP

## Detailed Documentation

See llms-full.txt for complete tool schemas, parameter descriptions, and example calls.
